title: Extraction and Quantification of Water Buffalo Milk Proteins via RP-HPLC-DAD and NIRS
author: Julián Andrés Castillo Vargas
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.pex-1423/v1

steps.title: On the day of the analysis,...
steps.instruction: On the day of the analysis, if the samples were frozen, thaw these at room temperature (25 °C).

steps.title: Take a 1 ml aliquot of...
steps.instruction: Take a 1 ml aliquot of milk and transfer it to a 2.2 mL Eppendorf tube using an automatic pipet.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of solution A to the 2
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of solution A to the 2.2 mL Eppendorf tube containing the milk sample (1:1 - sample: solution A).

steps.title: Shake each mixture in a vortex...
steps.instruction: Shake each mixture in a vortex for 10 s at medium speed.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture at room temperature (25 °C) for 1 h. Note: you can climate the room (25 °C) in which incubation is running by using an air conditioning unit. Standardization of the incubation conditions contributes to low variations across different processing days.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14200 x g for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14200 x g for 5 min.

steps.title: An upper fat layer is formed after centrifugation
steps.instruction: An upper fat layer is formed after centrifugation. Then, carefully remove it using a micro-spatula.

steps.title: Take 450 μl of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Take 450 μl of the supernatant and transfer it to a new 2.2 mL Eppendorf tube. To this same tube, add 1350 μl of solution B (1:3 - supernatant: solution B).

steps.title: Transfer 1 mL of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 mL of the mixture to a vial for HPLC analysis.

steps.title: Analyze the samples by RP-HPLC-DAD. Note
steps.instruction: Analyze the samples by RP-HPLC-DAD. Note: storage the samples under refrigeration at 4 °C until analysis.

steps.title: - Column temperature
steps.instruction: - Column temperature: 45 °C. - Injection volume: 5 µL. - Stationary phase: Hypersil GOLD C8 column (3 µm, 150 x 4.6 mm) equipped with Hypersil GOLD C8 guard column (5 µm, 10 mm). - Working wavelength: 214 nm. - Mode: gradient, constant flow: 0.500 mL/min (see the elution gradient in table 1, supplementary files).

steps.title: Determine the total protein and casein...
steps.instruction: Determine the total protein and casein contents in g/100 g milk in the sample using a NIRS equipment. Additionally, determine the milk sample density.

steps.title: Determine the peak area for each...
steps.instruction: Determine the peak area for each protein in the chromatogram, including caseins (κ-CN X2, α s2 -CN, κ-CN X1, α s1 -CN B, α s1 -CN A, and βγ-CN) and whey proteins (α-La and β-Lg) variants (Figure 1). Take in main that some casein variants could be absent in the sample.

steps.title: For calculation of the quantities of...
steps.instruction: For calculation of the quantities of casein variants (κ-CN X2, α s2 -CN, κ-CN X1, α s1 -CN B, α s1 -CN A, and βγ-CN), use the following procedure (use the supplementary material as well):

steps.title: Calculate the total area of casein...
steps.instruction: Calculate the total area of casein variant peaks (sum of peak areas).

steps.title: Calculate the proportion of each casein...
steps.instruction: Calculate the proportion of each casein variant according to the following equation: Proportion of casein variant = Peak area of casein variant/sum of peak areas of casein variants

steps.title: Calculate the concentration of each casein...
steps.instruction: Calculate the concentration of each casein variant in g/100 g milk: Concentration of casein variant (g/100 g milk) = Casein content (g/100 g milk) x Proportion of casein variant

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: calculation of the concentration of casein variant in g/L milk: Concentration of casein variant (g/L milk) = (Concentration of casein variant (g/100 g milk)) * (milk density (g/mL)) * 10

steps.title: For calculation of the quantities of...
steps.instruction: For calculation of the quantities of whey proteins variants (α-La and β-Lg), use the following procedure (use the supplementary material as well):

steps.title: Calculate the total area of whey...
steps.instruction: Calculate the total area of whey proteins variants (sum of peak areas).

steps.title: Calculate the proportion of each whey...
steps.instruction: Calculate the proportion of each whey protein variant according to the following equation: Proportion of whey protein variant = Peak area of whey protein variant/sum of peak areas of whey proteins

steps.title: Calculation of total whey protein
steps.instruction: Calculation of total whey protein: assuming that total milk protein = casein + whey protein, then: Whey protein content (g/100 g milk) = total milk protein (g/100 g milk) – casein (g/100 g milk).

steps.title: Calculate the concentration of each whey...
steps.instruction: Calculate the concentration of each whey protein variant in g/100 g milk: Concentration of whey protein variant (g/100 g milk) = Whey protein content (g/100 g milk) x Proportion of whey protein variant

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: calculation of the concentration of whey protein variants in g/L milk: Concentration of whey protein variant (g/L milk) = (Concentration of whey protein variant (g/100 g milk)) * (milk density (g/mL)) * 10

1. Take special care with homogenous sampling. In some cases, when milk was storage for long periods or milk is thawed-frozen, it tends to form solid particulate materials. If particulate material is formed, shake the milk’s container vigorously to take a representative sample. Otherwise, discard the sample. 2. The equilibrium program before each sample run, is strictly necessary to get a successfully milk protein separation. Check the flow maximum limits of your column before use. 3. If your HPLC equipment does not contain an autosampler module with liquid refrigerator, you can use a conventional refrigerator instead, and inject samples manually. The processed samples must keep storage at 4 °C.

steps.title: - Solutions and material preparation
steps.instruction: - Solutions and material preparation: 30 min - The extraction of protein variants: a batch of 24 samples: 101 min. - The total time of the chromatographic analysis (per sample): 53 min (13 min of equilibrium + 40 min of chromatographic run). - NIRS analysis: 5 min.

steps.title: See figures section for more details
steps.instruction: See figures section for more details.

steps.title: Figure 1
steps.instruction: Figure 1. Chromatogram profiles of buffaloes’ milk of different water buffaloes (a, b, c, and d). Consider that not all casein variants are expressed along the milk of different animals.

steps.title: Sample Of Calculation (Protocol)
steps.instruction: Sample Of Calculation (Protocol).Xlsx Datasheet for protein concentration calculations Table 1.Pdf Table 1. Chromatographic elution gradient
